Importance: core model dimensionality flags used downstream and by validate_args and core_transformer_config_from_args.

- _add_straggler_detector_args — Adds flags for straggler detection/logging. Importance: runtime/perf instrumentation.

- _add_one_logger_args — Adds flags controlling one_logger instrumentation. Importance: internal logging configuration.

- _add_ft_package_args — Adds Fault Tolerance package enable flag. Importance: optional FT behavior.

- _add_config_logger_args — Adds --config-logger-dir. Importance: where core configs can be dumped.

- _add_logging_args — Adds many logging flags, including:
  - --log-params-norm, --log-num-zeros-in-grad, --log-throughput, --log-progress
  - --timing-log-level, --tensorboard-log-interval, --tensorboard-queue-size
  - --log-timers-to-tensorboard, --no-log-loss-scale-to-tensorboard (dest='log_loss_scale_to_tensorboard')
  - --log-validation-ppl-to-tensorboard
  - wandb flags: --wandb-project, --wandb-exp-name, --wandb-save-dir
  - --logging-level
  Importance: Controls what is reported to TensorBoard/wandb and other logging sinks. If you want a new flag such as --log-cross-entropy it logically belongs here.

- _add_regularization_args — Adds regularization flags:
  - --attention-dropout, --hidden-dropout, --weight-decay, --start-weight-decay, --end-weight-decay, --weight-decay-incr-style
  - --clip-grad, --adam-beta1, --adam-beta2, --adam-eps, --sgd-momentum
  Importance: Regularization / optimizer hyperparameters. Global z-loss (a loss regularizer) could be placed here if you prefer grouping by regularization.

- _add_training_args — Adds training and runtime flags, including:
  - Batch sizing flags (--micro-batch-size, --global-batch-size, --rampup-batch-size, --decrease-batch-size-if-needed)
  - Recompute/fp/overlap flags, profiling, tp-comm-overlap flags
  - --deterministic-mode related options
  - --calculate-per-token-loss (scales cross entropy by number of non-padded tokens)
  - deprecated flags and others like --cross-entropy-loss-fusion (dest='cross_entropy_loss_fusion'), --use-flash-attn, --optimizer, --log-interval, --train-iters/train-samples
  Importance: Major location for training-related flags. If you want flags that control global loss behavior such as enabling z-loss (e.g., --add-z-loss, --z-loss-weight) these are also appropriate to add here. Logging of cross-entropy may be better added under _add_logging_args, but cross-entropy-related controls (e.g., how loss is computed) belong here.

- _add_initialization_args — Adds RNG and initialization flags (seed, data-parallel-random-init, init-method-std, init-method-xavier-uniform). Importance: initialization controls.

- _add_learning_rate_args — Adds LR schedule flags and decoupled lr options. Importance: training scheduler.

- _add_checkpointing_args — Adds checkpointing and resume flags, ckpt format flags, distributed checkpointing options, deprecations, and StrictHandling-related option mapping. Importance: checkpoint/resume behavior.

- _add_mixed_precision_args — Adds mixed-precision related flags: --fp16, --bf16, --loss-scale, dynamic scaling params, --fp32-residual-connection, --apply-query-key-layer-scaling (also forces attention_softmax_in_fp32), --attention-softmax-in-fp32, --accumulate-allreduce-grads-in-fp32, --fp16-lm-cross-entropy. Importance: affects dtype and some numerics; adding z-loss interactions with fp16/bf16 may require checks here or in validate_args.

- _add_distributed_args — Adds distribution and parallelism flags (tensor/pipeline/context/expert parallel sizes, lazy_mpu_init, overlap param gather flags, DDP options, local-rank, use-distributed-optimizer, etc.). Importance: parallelism configuration; validate_args uses many of these.

- _add_validation_args — Adds eval/test flags (eval-iters, eval-interval, test-mode, skip-train). Importance: evaluation behavior.

- _add_data_args — Adds data/dataloader flags (data-path, split, train/valid/test data path variants, seq-lengths, tokenizer options, eod-mask-loss, calculate number of threads, mock-data, vocab options). Importance: data and loss shaping; note the flag --eod-mask-loss (mask loss for end-of-document tokens) and --calculate-per-token-loss in training group already affect how loss is computed. If you add z-loss, consider interactions with eod-mask-loss and calculate-per-token-loss.

- _add_autoresume_args — Adds adlr-autoresume flags. Importance: job management.

- _add_biencoder_args — Adds biencoder/ICT/Siamese retriever flags. Importance: biencoder-specific.

- _add_vision_args — Adds vision model training flags. Importance: vision-related training.

- _add_moe_args — Adds MoE-specific flags. Of particular note:
  - --moe-aux-loss-coeff (aux loss scaling)
  - --moe-z-loss-coeff (type=float, default=None) — scaling coefficient for per-MoE z-loss
  - --moe-per-layer-logging (enable per-layer logging for MoE, supports auxiliary loss and z loss)
  Importance: MoE-specific z-loss is already present here as --moe-z-loss-coeff. If you want a global/non-MoE z-loss, add flags outside this function (e.g., in _add_regularization_args or _add_training_args). Also note moe-per-layer-logging exists and could be extended or reused.

- _add_mla_args — Adds multi-latent attention related args (q-lora-rank, kv-lora-rank, qk-head-dim, etc.). Importance: MLA-specific.

- _add_experimental_args — Adds experimental flags including --yaml-cfg. Importance: limited/experimental flags and YAML config support.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent wants to add z-loss related flags (for example: --add-z-loss, --z-loss-weight, --log-cross-entropy). Relevant points in this file:

- Where to define flags:
  - The CLI flags are registered in the many _add_* functions invoked by parse_args. To add new global z-loss flags, the most appropriate places are:
    - _add_training_args — logical for flags that change how training/loss is computed (e.g., --add-z-loss, --z-loss-weight).
    - _add_regularization_args — appropriate if z-loss is considered a regularizer.
    - _add_logging_args — appropriate for logging control flags like --log-cross-entropy.
  - There is already a MoE-specific z-loss flag: --moe-z-loss-coeff in _add_moe_args. Use its naming and pattern as a guideline for defaults and help text.

- Where new flags will be used/validated:
  - validate_args should be updated if there are constraints (e.g., ensure weights >= 0, or mutually exclusive options). validate_args is the place to enforce invariants or set derived values.
  - _print_args will automatically include new flags in printed summaries.
  - core_transformer_config_from_args is used to pass args to core TransformerConfig. If the z-loss impacts the transformer configuration, extend core_transformer_config_from_args to include the new fields. Otherwise, consumers elsewhere in the code base (loss computation code) must read args.<new_flag> to enable behavior.
  - Since the file already contains flags that affect loss computation (e.g., --calculate-per-token-loss, --eod-mask-loss, --cross-entropy-loss-fusion), ensure new z-loss flags are consistent with these.

- Other places to search in the codebase:
  - Search for usage of --moe-z-loss-coeff or args.moe_z_loss_coeff to find how MoE z-loss is implemented (to mirror naming/semantics or wiring).
  - Search for cross_entropy_loss_fusion and places where cross-entropy is logged (tensorboard/wandb hooks) to see how to wire a --log-cross-entropy flag into the logging pipeline.

Implementation checklist for the PR:
- Add CLI flags in the chosen _add_* function(s) (suggested: _add_training_args for behavior flags, _add_logging_args for logging flags).
- Add validation / default logic to validate_args if needed (e.g., non-negative coefficient).
- If z-loss must be propagated to core config (TransformerConfig), add mapping in core_transformer_config_from_args or ensure the code that computes the loss reads args directly.
- Update any logging components (search for tensorboard/wandb logging code) to honor --log-cross-entropy.
- Inspect and possibly mirror how --moe-z-loss-coeff is used to properly wire a global z-loss.

If you want, I can produce the exact code patch adding example flags (--add-z-loss, --z-loss-weight, --log-cross-entropy) in the recommended locations, and update validate_args with basic checks.

tool_calls.raw_result:
# Copyright (c) 2023, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.

from collections import OrderedDict
from typing import Dict, Literal, Optional

from torch import Tensor

from megatron.core import InferenceParams, tensor_parallel
from megatron.core.config_logger import has_config_logger_enabled, log_config_to_disk
from megatron.core.dist_checkpointing.mapping import ShardedStateDict
from megatron.core.models.common.embeddings.language_model_embedding import LanguageModelEmbedding
from megatron.core.models.common.embeddings.rotary_pos_embedding import RotaryEmbedding
from megatron.core.models.common.language_module.language_module import LanguageModule
from megatron.core.packed_seq_params import PackedSeqParams
from megatron.core.transformer.enums import ModelType
from megatron.core.transformer.spec_utils import ModuleSpec
from megatron.core.transformer.transformer_block import TransformerBlock
from megatron.core.transformer.transformer_config import TransformerConfig


class GPTModel(LanguageModule):
    """GPT Transformer language model.

    Args:
        config (TransformerConfig):
            Transformer config
        transformer_layer_spec (ModuleSpec):
            Specifies module to use for transformer layers
        vocab_size (int):
            Vocabulary size
        max_sequence_length (int):
            maximum size of sequence. This is used for positional embedding
        pre_process (bool, optional):
            Include embedding layer (used with pipeline parallelism). Defaults to True.
        post_process (bool, optional):
            Include an output layer (used with pipeline parallelism). Defaults to True.
        fp16_lm_cross_entropy (bool, optional):
            Defaults to False.
        parallel_output (bool, optional):
            Do not gather the outputs, keep them split across tensor
            parallel ranks. Defaults to True.
        share_embeddings_and_output_weights (bool, optional):
            When True, input embeddings and output logit weights are shared. Defaults to False.
        position_embedding_type (Literal[learned_absolute,rope], optional):
            Position embedding type.. Defaults to 'learned_absolute'.
        rotary_percent (float, optional):
            Percent of rotary dimension to use for rotary position embeddings.
            Ignored unless position_embedding_type is 'rope'. Defaults to 1.0.
        rotary_base (int, optional):
            Base period for rotary position embeddings. Ignored unless
            position_embedding_type is 'rope'.
            Defaults to 10000.
        seq_len_interpolation_factor (Optional[float], optional):
            scale of linearly interpolating RoPE for longer sequences.
            The value must be a float larger than 1.0. Defaults to None.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: TransformerConfig,
        transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec,
        vocab_size: int,
        max_sequence_length: int,
        pre_process: bool = True,
        post_process: bool = True,
        fp16_lm_cross_entropy: bool = False,
        parallel_output: bool = True,
        share_embeddings_and_output_weights: bool = False,
        position_embedding_type: Literal['learned_absolute', 'rope', 'none'] = 'learned_absolute',
        rotary_percent: float = 1.0,
        rotary_base: int = 10000,
        rope_scaling: bool = False,
        seq_len_interpolation_factor: Optional[float] = None,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(config=config)

        if has_config_logger_enabled(config):
            log_config_to_disk(config, locals(), prefix=type(self).__name__)

        self.transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec = transformer_layer_spec
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.max_sequence_length = max_sequence_length
        self.pre_process = pre_process
        self.post_process = post_process
        self.fp16_lm_cross_entropy = fp16_lm_cross_entropy
        self.parallel_output = parallel_output
        self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights = share_embeddings_and_output_weights
        self.position_embedding_type = position_embedding_type

        # megatron core pipelining currently depends on model type
        # TODO: remove this dependency ?
        self.model_type = ModelType.encoder_or_decoder

        # These 4 attributes are needed for TensorRT-LLM export.
        self.max_position_embeddings = max_sequence_length
        self.rotary_percent = rotary_percent
        self.rotary_base = rotary_base
        self.rotary_scaling = rope_scaling

        if self.pre_process:
            self.embedding = LanguageModelEmbedding(
                config=self.config,
                vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
                max_sequence_length=self.max_sequence_length,
                position_embedding_type=position_embedding_type,
            )

        if self.position_embedding_type == 'rope' and not self.config.multi_latent_attention:
            self.rotary_pos_emb = RotaryEmbedding(
                kv_channels=self.config.kv_channels,
                rotary_percent=rotary_percent,
                rotary_interleaved=self.config.rotary_interleaved,
                seq_len_interpolation_factor=seq_len_interpolation_factor,
                rotary_base=rotary_base,
                rope_scaling=rope_scaling,
                use_cpu_initialization=self.config.use_cpu_initialization,
            )

        # Transformer.
        self.decoder = TransformerBlock(
            config=self.config,
            spec=transformer_layer_spec,
            pre_process=self.pre_process,
            post_process=self.post_process,
        )

        # Output
        if post_process:
            if self.config.defer_embedding_wgrad_compute:
                # The embedding activation buffer preserves a reference to the input activations
                # of the final embedding projection layer GEMM. It will hold the activations for
                # all the micro-batches of a global batch for the last pipeline stage. Once we are
                # done with all the back props for all the microbatches for the last pipeline stage,
                # it will be in the pipeline flush stage. During this pipeline flush we use the
                # input activations stored in embedding activation buffer and gradient outputs
                # stored in gradient buffer to calculate the weight gradients for the embedding
                # final linear layer.
                self.embedding_activation_buffer = []
                self.grad_output_buffer = []
            else:
                self.embedding_activation_buffer = None
                self.grad_output_buffer = None

            self.output_layer = tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear(
                config.hidden_size,
                self.vocab_size,
                config=config,
                init_method=config.init_method,
                bias=False,
                skip_bias_add=False,
                gather_output=not self.parallel_output,
                skip_weight_param_allocation=self.pre_process
                and self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights,
                embedding_activation_buffer=self.embedding_activation_buffer,
                grad_output_buffer=self.grad_output_buffer,
            )

        if self.pre_process or self.post_process:
            self.setup_embeddings_and_output_layer()

        if has_config_logger_enabled(self.config):
            log_config_to_disk(
                self.config, self.state_dict(), prefix=f'{type(self).__name__}_init_ckpt'
            )

    def set_input_tensor(self, input_tensor: Tensor) -> None:
        """Sets input tensor to the model.

        See megatron.model.transformer.set_input_tensor()

        Args:
            input_tensor (Tensor): Sets the input tensor for the model.
        """
        # This is usually handled in schedules.py but some inference code still
        # gives us non-lists or None
        if not isinstance(input_tensor, list):
            input_tensor = [input_tensor]

        assert len(input_tensor) == 1, 'input_tensor should only be length 1 for gpt/bert'
        self.decoder.set_input_tensor(input_tensor[0])

    def forward(
        self,
        input_ids: Tensor,
        position_ids: Tensor,
        attention_mask: Tensor,
        decoder_input: Tensor = None,
        labels: Tensor = None,
        inference_params: InferenceParams = None,
        packed_seq_params: PackedSeqParams = None,
        extra_block_kwargs: dict = None,
        runtime_gather_output: Optional[bool] = None,
    ) -> Tensor:
        """Forward function of the GPT Model This function passes the input tensors
        through the embedding layer, and then the decoeder and finally into the post
        processing layer (optional).

        It either returns the Loss values if labels are given  or the final hidden units

        Args:
            runtime_gather_output (bool): Gather output at runtime. Default None means
                `parallel_output` arg in the constructor will be used.
        """
        # If decoder_input is provided (not None), then input_ids and position_ids are ignored.
        # Otherwise, apply embedding layer on input_ids and position_ids to get decoder_input.

        # Decoder embedding.
        if decoder_input is not None:
            pass
        elif self.pre_process:
            decoder_input = self.embedding(input_ids=input_ids, position_ids=position_ids)
        else:
            # intermediate stage of pipeline
            # decoder will get hidden_states from encoder.input_tensor
            decoder_input = None

        # Rotary positional embeddings (embedding is None for PP intermediate devices)
        rotary_pos_emb = None
        rotary_pos_cos = None
        rotary_pos_sin = None
        if self.position_embedding_type == 'rope' and not self.config.multi_latent_attention:
            if not self.training and self.config.flash_decode:
                # Flash decoding uses precomputed cos and sin for RoPE
                rotary_pos_cos, rotary_pos_sin = self.rotary_pos_emb.get_cos_sin(
                    inference_params.max_sequence_length
                )
            else:
                rotary_seq_len = self.rotary_pos_emb.get_rotary_seq_len(
                    inference_params, self.decoder, decoder_input, self.config, packed_seq_params
                )
                rotary_pos_emb = self.rotary_pos_emb(
                    rotary_seq_len,
                    packed_seq=packed_seq_params is not None
                    and packed_seq_params.qkv_format == 'thd',
                )

        # Run decoder.
        hidden_states = self.decoder(
            hidden_states=decoder_input,
            attention_mask=attention_mask,
            inference_params=inference_params,
            rotary_pos_emb=rotary_pos_emb,
            rotary_pos_cos=rotary_pos_cos,
            rotary_pos_sin=rotary_pos_sin,
            packed_seq_params=packed_seq_params,
            **(extra_block_kwargs or {}),
        )

        if not self.post_process:
            return hidden_states

        # logits and loss
        output_weight = None
        if self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights:
            output_weight = self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight()
        logits, _ = self.output_layer(
            hidden_states, weight=output_weight, runtime_gather_output=runtime_gather_output
        )

        if has_config_logger_enabled(self.config):
            payload = OrderedDict(
                {
                    'input_ids': input_ids,
                    'position_ids': position_ids,
                    'attention_mask': attention_mask,
                    'decoder_input': decoder_input,
                    'logits': logits,
                }
            )
            log_config_to_disk(self.config, payload, prefix='input_and_logits')

        if labels is None:
            # [s b h] => [b s h]
            return logits.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()

        loss = self.compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits)

        return loss

    def sharded_state_dict(
        self, prefix: str = '', sharded_offsets: tuple = (), metadata: Optional[Dict] = None
    ) -> ShardedStateDict:
        """Sharded state dict implementation for GPTModel backward-compatibility
        (removing extra state).

        Args:
            prefix (str): Module name prefix.
            sharded_offsets (tuple): PP related offsets, expected to be empty at this module level.
            metadata (Optional[Dict]): metadata controlling sharded state dict creation.

        Returns:
            ShardedStateDict: sharded state dict for the GPTModel
        """
        sharded_state_dict = super().sharded_state_dict(prefix, sharded_offsets, metadata)
        output_layer_extra_state_key = f'{prefix}output_layer._extra_state'

        # Old GPT checkpoints only stored the output layer weight key. So we remove the
        # _extra_state key but check that it doesn't contain any data anyway
        output_extra_state = sharded_state_dict.pop(output_layer_extra_state_key, None)
        assert not (
            output_extra_state and output_extra_state.data
        ), f'Expected output layer extra state to be empty, got: {output_extra_state}'

        return sharded_state_dict
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file defines the GPTModel class, a GPT-style Transformer language model implementation built on top of megatron.core's LanguageModule. It assembles embeddings, rotary positional embeddings, a TransformerBlock decoder, and an output projection, and it routes logits to loss computation (compute_language_model_loss).

### Architecture
- GPTModel composes lower-level modules: LanguageModelEmbedding (optional), RotaryEmbedding (optional), TransformerBlock as the decoder, and a tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear output projection.
- The forward pass: input_ids/position_ids → LanguageModelEmbedding (if pre_process) → optional RoPE calculation → TransformerBlock decoder → output_layer to get logits → compute_language_model_loss if labels provided.
- The model supports pipeline parallelism (pre_process/post_process flags), tensor parallelism for the output layer (parallel_output / runtime_gather_output), shared embeddings and output weights, deferred embedding gradient computation buffers, and RoPE configuration.
- Loss is delegated to compute_language_model_loss (inherited from LanguageModule), which is the place where cross-entropy is computed in the core model path.

### Functions & Classes
- GPTModel — class implementing the GPT Transformer language model. Central class in this file.

  - __init__(self, config: TransformerConfig, transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec, vocab_size: int, max_sequence_length: int, pre_process: bool = True, post_process: bool = True, fp16_lm_cross_entropy: bool = False, parallel_output: bool = True, share_embeddings_and_output_weights: bool = False, position_embedding_type: Literal['learned_absolute', 'rope', 'none'] = 'learned_absolute', rotary_percent: float = 1.0, rotary_base: int = 10000, rope_scaling: bool = False, seq_len_interpolation_factor: Optional[float] = None) -> None — constructs the GPTModel and wires up components.
    - Importance: sets all model-level configuration flags and creates submodules needed for forward/inference:
      - records config fields as attributes: transformer_layer_spec, vocab_size, max_sequence_length, pre_process, post_process, fp16_lm_cross_entropy, parallel_output, share_embeddings_and_output_weights, position_embedding_type, model_type, max_position_embeddings, rotary_percent, rotary_base, rotary_scaling.
      - If pre_process is True, creates self.embedding: LanguageModelEmbedding.
      - If position_embedding_type == 'rope', creates self.rotary_pos_emb: RotaryEmbedding (unless multi_latent_attention).
      - Creates self.decoder: TransformerBlock that contains transformer layers according to transformer_layer_spec.
      - If post_process is True, sets up embedding gradient buffers if config.defer_embedding_wgrad_compute and creates self.output_layer: tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear configured to gather output based on parallel_output and to potentially share weights with embeddings.
      - Calls setup_embeddings_and_output_layer() if pre_process or post_process — this method is inherited from the base class and links embeddings/output weights if shared.
      - Logs configuration if config logger enabled.

  - set_input_tensor(self, input_tensor: Tensor) -> None — sets the input tensor for pipeline-parallel intermediate stages.
    - Importance: compatibility with pipeline schedules. Accepts either a single Tensor or list of length 1; asserts length 1; forwards the tensor to self.decoder.set_input_tensor. Used when other pipeline stages set hidden states directly instead of embeddings.

  - forward(self, input_ids: Tensor, position_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor, decoder_input: Tensor = None, labels: Tensor = None, inference_params: InferenceParams = None, packed_seq_params: PackedSeqParams = None, extra_block_kwargs: dict = None, runtime_gather_output: Optional[bool] = None) -> Tensor — main forward pass.
    - Role:
      - If decoder_input is provided, embedding step is skipped; otherwise, if pre_process is True, computes decoder_input via self.embedding(input_ids, position_ids). If not pre_process (pipeline intermediate), decoder_input left as None.
      - Handles rotary position embedding computation: sets rotary_pos_emb / rotary_pos_cos / rotary_pos_sin depending on position_embedding_type, inference vs training, flash_decode, and packed_seq_params.
      - Calls self.decoder(...) with hidden_states=decoder_input and passes rotary-related tensors, inference_params, packed_seq_params, and extra_block_kwargs; receives hidden_states output from decoder.
      - If post_process is False, immediately returns hidden_states (used in pipeline intermediate stages).
      - For post_process True: obtains output_weight via self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight() if share_embeddings_and_output_weights is True; calls self.output_layer(hidden_states, weight=output_weight, runtime_gather_output=runtime_gather_output) to produce logits (and a second unused return).
      - If labels is None: returns logits.transpose(0, 1).contiguous() (shape change from [seq, batch, vocab] to [batch, seq, vocab]).
      - If labels provided: calls self.compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits) and returns the loss.
    - Importance: central runtime path. Note the exact call compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits) — this is where cross-entropy loss is invoked in the core model path (compute_language_model_loss is provided by the LanguageModule base class and is the expected modification point to add zloss alongside cross-entropy). Also respects fp16_lm_cross_entropy through inherited loss implementation.

  - sharded_state_dict(self, prefix: str = '', sharded_offsets: tuple = (), metadata: Optional[Dict] = None) -> ShardedStateDict — creates a sharded state dict for checkpointing with backward-compatibility adjustments.
    - Role:
      - Calls super().sharded_state_dict(prefix, sharded_offsets, metadata) to get a base sharded state dict.
      - Removes f'{prefix}output_layer._extra_state' key to maintain compatibility with old GPT checkpoints that only stored output weight key; asserts that the removed extra state is empty if it exists.
    - Importance: ensures backwards compatibility of checkpoint formats for GPTModel.

Other referenced methods/attributes (not defined here but important to know where behavior comes from):
- compute_language_model_loss — invoked in forward; implemented in base class LanguageModule (not in this file). This is the place where vocab_parallel_cross_entropy is expected to be used; to add zloss in the core model path, modify compute_language_model_loss or its internal call chain.
- shared_embedding_or_output_weight — used to get output projection weight when embeddings and output are shared; implemented in base class.
- setup_embeddings_and_output_layer — invoked in __init__ when pre_process or post_process is True; links embedding and output weights when required.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The agent is looking for where cross-entropy (vocab_parallel_cross_entropy) is called in the core model path so zloss can be added. In this file, the forward method is the entry point that produces logits and then calls compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits) to produce the loss. compute_language_model_loss is therefore the direct hook for loss computation; it is defined in LanguageModule (superclass), not in this file.
- Relevant lines to modify or inspect:
  - In forward: the call "loss = self.compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits)" — this is where to add zloss behavior or extend loss computation.
  - __init__ flags that may affect loss behavior: fp16_lm_cross_entropy (stored as self.fp16_lm_cross_entropy) — the loss routine may consult this flag.
  - output_layer creation: ColumnParallelLinear(..., gather_output=not self.parallel_output, ...) and the runtime_gather_output parameter in forward control whether logits are gathered; this affects how compute_language_model_loss will need to receive logits (gathered vs. sharded) when adding zloss or invoking vocab_parallel_cross_entropy.
- Summary recommendation: Do not change this file to insert vocab_parallel_cross_entropy directly; instead, modify compute_language_model_loss (in LanguageModule or wherever it is defined) to add zloss immediately around or after the existing cross-entropy calculation. Ensure handling of parallel/sharded logits via parallel_output/runtime_gather_output is respected when adding zloss.

## File: megatron/core/models/gpt/gpt_model.py

## Agent's reason for reading this file:

## What the agent expects to find:

## Source Code:
# Copyright (c) 2023, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.

from collections import OrderedDict
from typing import Dict, Literal, Optional

from torch import Tensor

from megatron.core import InferenceParams, tensor_parallel
from megatron.core.config_logger import has_config_logger_enabled, log_config_to_disk
from megatron.core.dist_checkpointing.mapping import ShardedStateDict
from megatron.core.models.common.embeddings.language_model_embedding import LanguageModelEmbedding
from megatron.core.models.common.embeddings.rotary_pos_embedding import RotaryEmbedding
from megatron.core.models.common.language_module.language_module import LanguageModule
from megatron.core.packed_seq_params import PackedSeqParams
from megatron.core.transformer.enums import ModelType
from megatron.core.transformer.spec_utils import ModuleSpec
from megatron.core.transformer.transformer_block import TransformerBlock
from megatron.core.transformer.transformer_config import TransformerConfig


class GPTModel(LanguageModule):
    """GPT Transformer language model.

    Args:
        config (TransformerConfig):
            Transformer config
        transformer_layer_spec (ModuleSpec):
            Specifies module to use for transformer layers
        vocab_size (int):
            Vocabulary size
        max_sequence_length (int):
            maximum size of sequence. This is used for positional embedding
        pre_process (bool, optional):
            Include embedding layer (used with pipeline parallelism). Defaults to True.
        post_process (bool, optional):
            Include an output layer (used with pipeline parallelism). Defaults to True.
        fp16_lm_cross_entropy (bool, optional):
            Defaults to False.
        parallel_output (bool, optional):
            Do not gather the outputs, keep them split across tensor
            parallel ranks. Defaults to True.
        share_embeddings_and_output_weights (bool, optional):
            When True, input embeddings and output logit weights are shared. Defaults to False.
        position_embedding_type (Literal[learned_absolute,rope], optional):
            Position embedding type.. Defaults to 'learned_absolute'.
        rotary_percent (float, optional):
            Percent of rotary dimension to use for rotary position embeddings.
            Ignored unless position_embedding_type is 'rope'. Defaults to 1.0.
        rotary_base (int, optional):
            Base period for rotary position embeddings. Ignored unless
            position_embedding_type is 'rope'.
            Defaults to 10000.
        seq_len_interpolation_factor (Optional[float], optional):
            scale of linearly interpolating RoPE for longer sequences.
            The value must be a float larger than 1.0. Defaults to None.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: TransformerConfig,
        transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec,
        vocab_size: int,
        max_sequence_length: int,
        pre_process: bool = True,
        post_process: bool = True,
        fp16_lm_cross_entropy: bool = False,
        parallel_output: bool = True,
        share_embeddings_and_output_weights: bool = False,
        position_embedding_type: Literal['learned_absolute', 'rope', 'none'] = 'learned_absolute',
        rotary_percent: float = 1.0,
        rotary_base: int = 10000,
        rope_scaling: bool = False,
        seq_len_interpolation_factor: Optional[float] = None,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(config=config)

        if has_config_logger_enabled(config):
            log_config_to_disk(config, locals(), prefix=type(self).__name__)

        self.transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec = transformer_layer_spec
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.max_sequence_length = max_sequence_length
        self.pre_process = pre_process
        self.post_process = post_process
        self.fp16_lm_cross_entropy = fp16_lm_cross_entropy
        self.parallel_output = parallel_output
        self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights = share_embeddings_and_output_weights
        self.position_embedding_type = position_embedding_type

        # megatron core pipelining currently depends on model type
        # TODO: remove this dependency ?
        self.model_type = ModelType.encoder_or_decoder

        # These 4 attributes are needed for TensorRT-LLM export.
        self.max_position_embeddings = max_sequence_length
        self.rotary_percent = rotary_percent
        self.rotary_base = rotary_base
        self.rotary_scaling = rope_scaling

        if self.pre_process:
            self.embedding = LanguageModelEmbedding(
                config=self.config,
                vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
                max_sequence_length=self.max_sequence_length,
                position_embedding_type=position_embedding_type,
            )

        if self.position_embedding_type == 'rope' and not self.config.multi_latent_attention:
            self.rotary_pos_emb = RotaryEmbedding(
                kv_channels=self.config.kv_channels,
                rotary_percent=rotary_percent,
                rotary_interleaved=self.config.rotary_interleaved,
                seq_len_interpolation_factor=seq_len_interpolation_factor,
                rotary_base=rotary_base,
                rope_scaling=rope_scaling,
                use_cpu_initialization=self.config.use_cpu_initialization,
            )

        # Transformer.
        self.decoder = TransformerBlock(
            config=self.config,
            spec=transformer_layer_spec,
            pre_process=self.pre_process,
            post_process=self.post_process,
        )

        # Output
        if post_process:
            if self.config.defer_embedding_wgrad_compute:
                # The embedding activation buffer preserves a reference to the input activations
                # of the final embedding projection layer GEMM. It will hold the activations for
                # all the micro-batches of a global batch for the last pipeline stage. Once we are
                # done with all the back props for all the microbatches for the last pipeline stage,
                # it will be in the pipeline flush stage. During this pipeline flush we use the
                # input activations stored in embedding activation buffer and gradient outputs
                # stored in gradient buffer to calculate the weight gradients for the embedding
                # final linear layer.
                self.embedding_activation_buffer = []
                self.grad_output_buffer = []
            else:
                self.embedding_activation_buffer = None
                self.grad_output_buffer = None

            self.output_layer = tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear(
                config.hidden_size,
                self.vocab_size,
                config=config,
                init_method=config.init_method,
                bias=False,
                skip_bias_add=False,
                gather_output=not self.parallel_output,
                skip_weight_param_allocation=self.pre_process
                and self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights,
                embedding_activation_buffer=self.embedding_activation_buffer,
                grad_output_buffer=self.grad_output_buffer,
            )

        if self.pre_process or self.post_process:
            self.setup_embeddings_and_output_layer()

        if has_config_logger_enabled(self.config):
            log_config_to_disk(
                self.config, self.state_dict(), prefix=f'{type(self).__name__}_init_ckpt'
            )

    def set_input_tensor(self, input_tensor: Tensor) -> None:
        """Sets input tensor to the model.

        See megatron.model.transformer.set_input_tensor()

        Args:
            input_tensor (Tensor): Sets the input tensor for the model.
        """
        # This is usually handled in schedules.py but some inference code still
        # gives us non-lists or None
        if not isinstance(input_tensor, list):
            input_tensor = [input_tensor]

        assert len(input_tensor) == 1, 'input_tensor should only be length 1 for gpt/bert'
        self.decoder.set_input_tensor(input_tensor[0])

    def forward(
        self,
        input_ids: Tensor,
        position_ids: Tensor,
        attention_mask: Tensor,
        decoder_input: Tensor = None,
        labels: Tensor = None,
        inference_params: InferenceParams = None,
        packed_seq_params: PackedSeqParams = None,
        extra_block_kwargs: dict = None,
        runtime_gather_output: Optional[bool] = None,
    ) -> Tensor:
        """Forward function of the GPT Model This function passes the input tensors
        through the embedding layer, and then the decoeder and finally into the post
        processing layer (optional).

        It either returns the Loss values if labels are given  or the final hidden units

        Args:
            runtime_gather_output (bool): Gather output at runtime. Default None means
                `parallel_output` arg in the constructor will be used.
        """
        # If decoder_input is provided (not None), then input_ids and position_ids are ignored.
        # Otherwise, apply embedding layer on input_ids and position_ids to get decoder_input.

        # Decoder embedding.
        if decoder_input is not None:
            pass
        elif self.pre_process:
            decoder_input = self.embedding(input_ids=input_ids, position_ids=position_ids)
        else:
            # intermediate stage of pipeline
            # decoder will get hidden_states from encoder.input_tensor
            decoder_input = None

        # Rotary positional embeddings (embedding is None for PP intermediate devices)
        rotary_pos_emb = None
        rotary_pos_cos = None
        rotary_pos_sin = None
        if self.position_embedding_type == 'rope' and not self.config.multi_latent_attention:
            if not self.training and self.config.flash_decode:
                # Flash decoding uses precomputed cos and sin for RoPE
                rotary_pos_cos, rotary_pos_sin = self.rotary_pos_emb.get_cos_sin(
                    inference_params.max_sequence_length
                )
            else:
                rotary_seq_len = self.rotary_pos_emb.get_rotary_seq_len(
                    inference_params, self.decoder, decoder_input, self.config, packed_seq_params
                )
                rotary_pos_emb = self.rotary_pos_emb(
                    rotary_seq_len,
                    packed_seq=packed_seq_params is not None
                    and packed_seq_params.qkv_format == 'thd',
                )

        # Run decoder.
        hidden_states = self.decoder(
            hidden_states=decoder_input,
            attention_mask=attention_mask,
            inference_params=inference_params,
            rotary_pos_emb=rotary_pos_emb,
            rotary_pos_cos=rotary_pos_cos,
            rotary_pos_sin=rotary_pos_sin,
            packed_seq_params=packed_seq_params,
            **(extra_block_kwargs or {}),
        )

        if not self.post_process:
            return hidden_states

        # logits and loss
        output_weight = None
        if self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights:
            output_weight = self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight()
        logits, _ = self.output_layer(
            hidden_states, weight=output_weight, runtime_gather_output=runtime_gather_output
        )

        if has_config_logger_enabled(self.config):
            payload = OrderedDict(
                {
                    'input_ids': input_ids,
                    'position_ids': position_ids,
                    'attention_mask': attention_mask,
                    'decoder_input': decoder_input,
                    'logits': logits,
                }
            )
            log_config_to_disk(self.config, payload, prefix='input_and_logits')

        if labels is None:
            # [s b h] => [b s h]
            return logits.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()

        loss = self.compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits)

        return loss

    def sharded_state_dict(
        self, prefix: str = '', sharded_offsets: tuple = (), metadata: Optional[Dict] = None
    ) -> ShardedStateDict:
        """Sharded state dict implementation for GPTModel backward-compatibility
        (removing extra state).

        Args:
            prefix (str): Module name prefix.
            sharded_offsets (tuple): PP related offsets, expected to be empty at this module level.
            metadata (Optional[Dict]): metadata controlling sharded state dict creation.

        Returns:
            ShardedStateDict: sharded state dict for the GPTModel
        """
        sharded_state_dict = super().sharded_state_dict(prefix, sharded_offsets, metadata)
        output_layer_extra_state_key = f'{prefix}output_layer._extra_state'

        # Old GPT checkpoints only stored the output layer weight key. So we remove the
        # _extra_state key but check that it doesn't contain any data anyway
        output_extra_state = sharded_state_dict.pop(output_layer_extra_state_key, None)
        assert not (
            output_extra_state and output_extra_state.data
        ), f'Expected output layer extra state to be empty, got: {output_extra_state}'

        return sharded_state_dict

### File Overview
This file defines the GPTModel class, a GPT-style Transformer language model implementation built on top of megatron.core's LanguageModule. It assembles embeddings, rotary positional embeddings, a TransformerBlock decoder, and an output projection, and it routes logits to loss computation (compute_language_model_loss).

### Architecture
- GPTModel composes lower-level modules: LanguageModelEmbedding (optional), RotaryEmbedding (optional), TransformerBlock as the decoder, and a tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear output projection.
- The forward pass: input_ids/position_ids → LanguageModelEmbedding (if pre_process) → optional RoPE calculation → TransformerBlock decoder → output_layer to get logits → compute_language_model_loss if labels provided.
- The model supports pipeline parallelism (pre_process/post_process flags), tensor parallelism for the output layer (parallel_output / runtime_gather_output), shared embeddings and output weights, deferred embedding gradient computation buffers, and RoPE configuration.
- Loss is delegated to compute_language_model_loss (inherited from LanguageModule), which is the place where cross-entropy is computed in the core model path.

### Functions & Classes
- GPTModel — class implementing the GPT Transformer language model. Central class in this file.

  - __init__(self, config: TransformerConfig, transformer_layer_spec: ModuleSpec, vocab_size: int, max_sequence_length: int, pre_process: bool = True, post_process: bool = True, fp16_lm_cross_entropy: bool = False, parallel_output: bool = True, share_embeddings_and_output_weights: bool = False, position_embedding_type: Literal['learned_absolute', 'rope', 'none'] = 'learned_absolute', rotary_percent: float = 1.0, rotary_base: int = 10000, rope_scaling: bool = False, seq_len_interpolation_factor: Optional[float] = None) -> None — constructs the GPTModel and wires up components.
    - Importance: sets all model-level configuration flags and creates submodules needed for forward/inference:
      - records config fields as attributes: transformer_layer_spec, vocab_size, max_sequence_length, pre_process, post_process, fp16_lm_cross_entropy, parallel_output, share_embeddings_and_output_weights, position_embedding_type, model_type, max_position_embeddings, rotary_percent, rotary_base, rotary_scaling.
      - If pre_process is True, creates self.embedding: LanguageModelEmbedding.
      - If position_embedding_type == 'rope', creates self.rotary_pos_emb: RotaryEmbedding (unless multi_latent_attention).
      - Creates self.decoder: TransformerBlock that contains transformer layers according to transformer_layer_spec.
      - If post_process is True, sets up embedding gradient buffers if config.defer_embedding_wgrad_compute and creates self.output_layer: tensor_parallel.ColumnParallelLinear configured to gather output based on parallel_output and to potentially share weights with embeddings.
      - Calls setup_embeddings_and_output_layer() if pre_process or post_process — this method is inherited from the base class and links embeddings/output weights if shared.
      - Logs configuration if config logger enabled.

  - set_input_tensor(self, input_tensor: Tensor) -> None — sets the input tensor for pipeline-parallel intermediate stages.
    - Importance: compatibility with pipeline schedules. Accepts either a single Tensor or list of length 1; asserts length 1; forwards the tensor to self.decoder.set_input_tensor. Used when other pipeline stages set hidden states directly instead of embeddings.

  - forward(self, input_ids: Tensor, position_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor, decoder_input: Tensor = None, labels: Tensor = None, inference_params: InferenceParams = None, packed_seq_params: PackedSeqParams = None, extra_block_kwargs: dict = None, runtime_gather_output: Optional[bool] = None) -> Tensor — main forward pass.
    - Role:
      - If decoder_input is provided, embedding step is skipped; otherwise, if pre_process is True, computes decoder_input via self.embedding(input_ids, position_ids). If not pre_process (pipeline intermediate), decoder_input left as None.
      - Handles rotary position embedding computation: sets rotary_pos_emb / rotary_pos_cos / rotary_pos_sin depending on position_embedding_type, inference vs training, flash_decode, and packed_seq_params.
      - Calls self.decoder(...) with hidden_states=decoder_input and passes rotary-related tensors, inference_params, packed_seq_params, and extra_block_kwargs; receives hidden_states output from decoder.
      - If post_process is False, immediately returns hidden_states (used in pipeline intermediate stages).
      - For post_process True: obtains output_weight via self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight() if share_embeddings_and_output_weights is True; calls self.output_layer(hidden_states, weight=output_weight, runtime_gather_output=runtime_gather_output) to produce logits (and a second unused return).
      - If labels is None: returns logits.transpose(0, 1).contiguous() (shape change from [seq, batch, vocab] to [batch, seq, vocab]).
      - If labels provided: calls self.compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits) and returns the loss.
    - Importance: central runtime path. Note the exact call compute_language_model_loss(labels, logits) — this is where cross-entropy loss is invoked in the core model path (compute_language_model_loss is provided by the LanguageModule base class and is the expected modification point to add zloss alongside cross-entropy). Also respects fp16_lm_cross_entropy through inherited loss implementation.

  - sharded_state_dict(self, prefix: str = '', sharded_offsets: tuple = (), metadata: Optional[Dict] = None) -> ShardedStateDict — creates a sharded state dict for checkpointing with backward-compatibility adjustments.
    - Role:
      - Calls super().sharded_state_dict(prefix, sharded_offsets, metadata) to get a base sharded state dict.
      - Removes f'{prefix}output_layer._extra_state' key to maintain compatibility with old GPT checkpoints that only stored output weight key; asserts that the removed extra state is empty if it exists.
    - Importance: ensures backwards compatibility of checkpoint formats for GPTModel.

Other referenced methods/attributes (not defined here but important to know where behavior comes from):

# Copyright (c) 2024, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.
import logging
from typing import Optional, Tuple

import torch
from torch import Tensor

from megatron.core import parallel_state, tensor_parallel
from megatron.core.dist_checkpointing.mapping import ShardedStateDict
from megatron.core.fusions.fused_cross_entropy import fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy
from megatron.core.transformer.module import MegatronModule
from megatron.core.transformer.transformer_config import TransformerConfig
from megatron.core.utils import make_tp_sharded_tensor_for_checkpoint


class LanguageModule(MegatronModule):
    """Base language module that has common helper functions used across GPT, BERT etc.

    Args:
        config (TransformerConfig): Input transformer config for the model
    """

    def __init__(self, config: TransformerConfig) -> None:
        super().__init__(config=config)

    def compute_language_model_loss(self, labels: Tensor, logits: Tensor) -> Tensor:
        """Computes the language model loss (Cross entropy across vocabulary)

        Args:
            labels (Tensor): The labels of dimension [batch size, seq length]
            logits (Tensor): The final logits returned by the output layer of the transformer model

        Returns:
            Tensor: Loss tensor of dimensions [batch size, sequence_length]
        """
        # [b s] => [s b]
        labels = labels.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
        if self.config.cross_entropy_loss_fusion:
            loss = fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy(logits, labels)
        else:
            loss = tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy(logits, labels)

        # [s b] => [b, s]
        loss = loss.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
        return loss

    def setup_embeddings_and_output_layer(self) -> None:
        """Sets up embedding layer in first stage and output layer in last stage.

        This function initalizes word embeddings in the final stage when we are
        using pipeline parallelism and sharing word embeddings, and sets up param
        attributes on the embedding and output layers.
        """

        # Set `is_embedding_or_output_parameter` attribute.
        if self.pre_process:
            self.embedding.word_embeddings.weight.is_embedding_or_output_parameter = True
        if self.post_process and self.output_layer.weight is not None:
            self.output_layer.weight.is_embedding_or_output_parameter = True

        if not self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights:
            return

        if parallel_state.get_pipeline_model_parallel_world_size() == 1:
            # Zero out wgrad if sharing embeddings between two layers on same
            # pipeline stage to make sure grad accumulation into main_grad is
            # correct and does not include garbage values (e.g., from torch.empty).
            self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight().zero_out_wgrad = True
            return

        if parallel_state.is_pipeline_first_stage() and self.pre_process and not self.post_process:
            self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight().shared_embedding = True

        if self.post_process and not self.pre_process:
            assert not parallel_state.is_pipeline_first_stage()
            # set word_embeddings weights to 0 here, then copy first
            # stage's weights using all_reduce below.
            self.output_layer.weight.data.fill_(0)
            self.output_layer.weight.shared = True
            self.output_layer.weight.shared_embedding = True

        # Parameters are shared between the word embeddings layers, and the
        # heads at the end of the model. In a pipelined setup with more than
        # one stage, the initial embedding layer and the head are on different
        # workers, so we do the following:
        # 1. Create a second copy of word_embeddings on the last stage, with
        #    initial parameters of 0.0.
        # 2. Do an all-reduce between the first and last stage to ensure that
        #    the two copies of word_embeddings start off with the same
        #    parameter values.
        # 3. In the training loop, before an all-reduce between the grads of
        #    the two word_embeddings layers to ensure that every applied weight
        #    update is the same on both stages.

        # Ensure that first and last stages have the same initial parameter
        # values.
        if torch.distributed.is_initialized():
            if parallel_state.is_rank_in_embedding_group():
                weight = self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight()
                weight.data = weight.data.cuda()
                torch.distributed.all_reduce(
                    weight.data, group=parallel_state.get_embedding_group()
                )

        elif not getattr(LanguageModule, "embedding_warning_printed", False):
            logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
                "Distributed processes aren't initialized, so the output layer "
                "is not initialized with weights from the word embeddings. "
                "If you are just manipulating a model this is fine, but "
                "this needs to be handled manually. If you are training "
                "something is definitely wrong."
            )
            LanguageModule.embedding_warning_printed = True

    def shared_embedding_or_output_weight(self) -> Tensor:
        """Gets the emedding weight or output logit weights when share embedding and output weights set to True.

        Returns:
            Tensor: During pre processing it returns the input embeddings weight while during post processing it returns the final output layers weight
        """
        if self.pre_process:
            return self.embedding.word_embeddings.weight
        elif self.post_process:
            return self.output_layer.weight
        return None

    def sharded_state_dict(
        self,
        prefix: str = '',
        sharded_offsets: Tuple[Tuple[int, int, int]] = (),
        metadata: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> ShardedStateDict:
        """Sharded state dict implementation that handles the output layer weights tying.

        Args:
            prefix (str): Module name prefix.
            sharded_offsets (tuple): PP related offsets, expected to be empty at this module level.
            metadata (Optional[Dict]): metadata controlling sharded state dict creation.

        Returns:
            ShardedStateDict: sharded state dict for the LanguageModel
        """
        assert not sharded_offsets, "Unexpected sharded offsets"
        sharded_state_dict = super().sharded_state_dict(prefix, sharded_offsets, metadata)

        first_stage_word_emb_key = f'{prefix}embedding.word_embeddings.weight'
        output_layer_weight_key = f'{prefix}output_layer.weight'
        output_layer_bias_key = f'{prefix}output_layer.bias'

        if self.share_embeddings_and_output_weights:
            self.tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict(
                sharded_state_dict, output_layer_weight_key, first_stage_word_emb_key
            )
        elif self.post_process:
            # Make sure the output layer follows the embeddings padding logic
            sharded_state_dict[output_layer_weight_key].allow_shape_mismatch = True

        # Regardless of sharing the output weights with embeddings, we must handle the bias padding
        if self.post_process and output_layer_bias_key in sharded_state_dict:
            sharded_state_dict[output_layer_bias_key].allow_shape_mismatch = True

        return sharded_state_dict

    def tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict(
        self,
        sharded_state_dict: ShardedStateDict,
        output_layer_weight_key: str,
        first_stage_word_emb_key: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Ties the embedding and output weights in a given sharded state dict.

        Args:
            sharded_state_dict (ShardedStateDict): state dict with the weight to tie
            output_layer_weight_key (str): key of the output layer weight in the state dict.
                This entry will be replaced with a tied version
            first_stage_word_emb_key (str): this must be the same as the
                ShardedTensor.key of the first stage word embeddings.

        Returns: None, acts in-place
        """
        if not self.post_process:
            # No output layer
            assert output_layer_weight_key not in sharded_state_dict, sharded_state_dict.keys()
            return

        if self.pre_process:
            # Output layer is equivalent to the embedding already
            return

        # Replace the default output layer with a one sharing the weights with the embedding
        del sharded_state_dict[output_layer_weight_key]
        tensor = self.shared_embedding_or_output_weight()
        last_stage_word_emb_replica_id = (
            1,  # copy of first stage embedding
            0,
            parallel_state.get_data_parallel_rank(with_context_parallel=True),
        )

        sharded_state_dict[output_layer_weight_key] = make_tp_sharded_tensor_for_checkpoint(
            tensor=tensor,
            key=first_stage_word_emb_key,
            replica_id=last_stage_word_emb_replica_id,
            allow_shape_mismatch=True,
        )

### File Overview
This file defines LanguageModule, a base MegatronModule with utilities common to language models (GPT, BERT). Its primary responsibilities shown here are computing the language-model cross-entropy loss and handling embedding/output-layer setup and sharded checkpointing when embeddings and the output layer are tied.

### Architecture
- LanguageModule extends MegatronModule and is initialized with a TransformerConfig.
- compute_language_model_loss is the single place this file computes per-token cross-entropy loss; it chooses between a fused CCE implementation and a tensor-parallel implementation based on config.
- setup_embeddings_and_output_layer, shared_embedding_or_output_weight, sharded_state_dict, and tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict manage embedding / output weight sharing, pipeline-parallel initialization/all-reduce behavior, and construction of a ShardedStateDict for checkpointing.
- Parallel/TP behavior is driven via parallel_state, tensor_parallel and torch.distributed interactions. The fused cross-entropy implementation is imported from megatron.core.fusions.fused_cross_entropy.

### Functions & Classes
- LanguageModule (class) — Subclass of MegatronModule that provides shared utilities for language models. Important because most model variants (GPT/BERT) will inherit these methods for loss computation and weight-sharing behavior.

- __init__(self, config: TransformerConfig) — Constructor; simply calls the MegatronModule constructor with the provided config. Establishes that the instance carries TransformerConfig and inherits MegatronModule behavior.

- compute_language_model_loss(self, labels: Tensor, logits: Tensor) -> Tensor — This is the function of highest relevance for the agent's goal:
  - Purpose: "Computes the language model loss (Cross entropy across vocabulary)".
  - Input expectations: labels shaped [batch size, seq length] (documented) and logits (final transformer output). The implementation immediately transposes labels to [seq length, batch] (labels = labels.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()) before calling cross-entropy.
  - Cross-entropy dispatch:
    - If self.config.cross_entropy_loss_fusion is truthy, it calls fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy(logits, labels).
    - Otherwise it calls tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy(logits, labels).
  - Postprocessing: The returned loss is transposed back ([s b] => [b, s]) and returned.
  - Return: Tensor of dimensions [batch size, sequence_length].
  - Importance: This is exactly where the file computes CE and selects the implementation; to add z-loss computation and separate logging of CE and z-loss, this method is the primary modification target. Note: currently it returns only the combined loss tensor and performs no separate logging or z-loss math.

- setup_embeddings_and_output_layer(self) -> None — Configures embedding and output-layer parameters, especially when share_embeddings_and_output_weights is True and in pipelined setups:
  - Sets attribute is_embedding_or_output_parameter on embedding/output weights where applicable.
  - If sharing and pipeline size is 1, sets zero_out_wgrad on the shared weight.
  - Handles first/last pipeline stage flagging (shared_embedding, shared, shared_embedding flags) and sets output_layer.weight to zeros on last stage if needed.
  - If torch.distributed.is_initialized() and rank is part of embedding group, makes a CUDA copy and performs an all_reduce to sync initial weights across embedding group.
  - Emits a one-time warning if distributed is not initialized.
  - Importance: Ensures correct initial weights and flags for embedding-output tying under pipeline parallelism and distributed training.

- shared_embedding_or_output_weight(self) -> Tensor — Returns the appropriate weight Tensor when embedding and output weights are shared:
  - Returns self.embedding.word_embeddings.weight if self.pre_process is True.
  - Returns self.output_layer.weight if self.post_process is True.
  - Returns None otherwise.
  - Importance: Central helper for weight tying logic (used by setup_embeddings_and_output_layer and tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict).

- sharded_state_dict(self, prefix: str = '', sharded_offsets: Tuple[Tuple[int, int, int]] = (), metadata: Optional[dict] = None) -> ShardedStateDict — Produces a ShardedStateDict for checkpointing:
  - Calls super().sharded_state_dict(...) and then adjusts entries related to embedding/output weights and bias.
  - If share_embeddings_and_output_weights is True, calls tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict to replace output weight entry with a tied entry referring to the first-stage embedding key.
  - If not sharing but post_process, marks output_layer.weight as allow_shape_mismatch to accommodate padding.
  - For post_process, ensures output_layer.bias (if present) also allows shape mismatch.
  - Importance: Ensures checkpoint entries reflect embedding/output weight tying and padding mismatches across pipeline stages.

- tie_embeddings_and_output_weights_state_dict(self, sharded_state_dict: ShardedStateDict, output_layer_weight_key: str, first_stage_word_emb_key: str) -> None — Mutates the provided ShardedStateDict to make the output_layer.weight entry a sharded tensor tied to the first-stage embedding:
  - If not self.post_process: asserts no output layer entry in the state dict and returns.
  - If self.pre_process: nothing to do since output layer is already equivalent to embedding.
  - Otherwise deletes sharded_state_dict[output_layer_weight_key], obtains the shared tensor via shared_embedding_or_output_weight(), sets a replica_id tuple (1, 0, data-parallel-rank-with-context-parallel), and inserts a sharded tensor via make_tp_sharded_tensor_for_checkpoint with allow_shape_mismatch=True.
  - Importance: Implements the actual mapping in checkpoint metadata so that tied weights are correctly represented across pipeline-parallel checkpoints.

- Top-level imports referenced (important to modifications):
  - fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy (imported from megatron.core.fusions.fused_cross_entropy) — used when config.cross_entropy_loss_fusion is set.
  - tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy — used as fallback CE implementation.
  - parallel_state, tensor_parallel, make_tp_sharded_tensor_for_checkpoint, ShardedStateDict, MegatronModule, TransformerConfig — used across embedding and sharded-checkpoint logic.
  - Note: compute_language_model_loss relies directly on fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy and tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy for CE computation.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The agent wants to "Inspect compute_language_model_loss implementation to add z-loss and logging of CE and z-loss separately." compute_language_model_loss is the exact method to change:
  - It currently transposes labels and calls either fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy or tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy, then transposes and returns a per-token loss tensor. There is no z-loss computation or separate logging.
  - To add z-loss, modify compute_language_model_loss to compute the log-normalizer (log-sum-exp over vocab) from logits (taking into account tensor-parallel vocabulary sharding if necessary) and compute a z-loss term (e.g., alpha * (log_z)**2) per token, then combine CE and z-loss appropriately, and return or emit separate CE and z-loss metrics.
  - Because the code dispatches to fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy and tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy, be aware:
    - If using fused_vocab_parallel_cross_entropy, you may need to update that fused routine (megaton.core.fusions.fused_cross_entropy) to also return log-normalizer or z information, or compute z-loss externally from logits while ensuring correctness under tensor-parallel vocabulary sharding.
    - If using tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy, examine its API/behavior (it is called as tensor_parallel.vocab_parallel_cross_entropy(logits, labels)) to see whether it exposes per-token logZ or provides logits post-processing; otherwise compute logZ from logits before calling it.
  - There is no existing logging API usage in compute_language_model_loss; decide whether to:
    - Return a tuple (loss_tensor, logging_dict) or (ce_loss, z_loss) and update call sites that expect the single loss tensor, or
    - Keep returning combined loss but expose per-term values via a side channel (e.g., set attributes on the module, call some logger, or integrate with the training loop reporting mechanism).
  - Also note shape conventions: compute_language_model_loss transposes labels [b, s] -> [s, b] and expects the CE functions to accept logits and labels in that arrangement; any z-loss computation must use consistent dims and handle tensor-parallel vocabulary sharding.

These are the precise locations and constraints to account for when adding z-loss computation and separate CE/z-loss logging.
